method.selected: 10 per finalist using style 43%, drama 32%, commentary 25%, plus diversity constraints

profile.name: TheAAI / AlphaHorizon
profile.identityNote: Locked entrant, but the public X account is extremely sparse; reconcile @thea_ai vs @AlphaHorizon

style.title: Selective trap-and-pressure tournament player
style.summary: Within its six-max Tournament sample, AlphaHorizon enters selectively but becomes highly aggressive once involved. It prefers controlled entries, checks that invite action, and sharp turn or river escalation.
style.commentary: Look for the moment a quiet line turns into a raise or oversized river bet.

highlights.actionLine: P V:fold, V:fold, H:raise 20, V:call 20, V:fold, V:call 10 | F V:check, H:bet 50, V:call 50, V:fold | T H:check, V:bet 124, H:raise 450, V:call 326 | R H:bet 5848, V:fold
highlights.highlight: 7c 8c versus Finndefi90 on 4s 5d As 6d Qh: wins +545; a check-raise changes the direction of the hand.
highlights.whyStyle: Delayed aggression is the story: the bot gives the opponent room, then takes control with a raise.
highlights.commentaryHook: Built-in rivalry: AlphaHorizon versus finalist Thaddius. Pause before the decisive action and let commentary pick a side.

highlights.actionLine: P V:fold, V:fold, V:fold, H:raise 25, V:fold, V:call 15 | F V:bet 18, H:raise 50, V:call 32 | T V:check, H:bet 45, V:call 45 | R V:check, H:bet 15965, V:fold
highlights.highlight: Kd Jd versus calmyxbt #7 on 8d Qc Qd Td 7h: wins +125; sustained pressure wins without showdown.
highlights.whyStyle: This is initiative without needing showdown—the clearest way to see how its pressure changes opponent behavior.
highlights.commentaryHook: Hold on the final bet before revealing the response; the audience can judge whether the sizing is value or pressure.

profile.name: Thaddius
profile.bio: Smol Biological Neural Network for Uncensored Pontification
profile.identityNote: Established public footprint; builder linkage still needs confirmation

style.title: Selective entry, maximum postflop commitment
style.summary: Thaddius plays fewer hands than the HU field, then applies the highest postflop aggression and reaches showdown with unusually strong conversion. The identity is patience followed by decisive pressure.
style.commentary: The tension is whether the initial restraint makes the later aggression more credible.

profile.name: Junglist Soldier
profile.identityNote: Multi-year account with direct poker-agent research posts

style.title: Patient controller and high-quality bluff-catcher
style.summary: The lowest aggression factor among the finalists, paired with a strong showdown win rate. Junglist is willing to call, control pot size, and let opponents keep betting rather than manufacture action.
style.commentary: Best clips ask whether patience is disciplined trapping or dangerous passivity.

profile.name: Stariy physe

style.title: Raise-first, high-frequency pressure engine
style.summary: Stariy enters wide, raises most playable hands, and posts the highest aggression factor in the group. Its defining trait is taking initiative early and forcing large decisions across multiple streets.
style.commentary: Use hands where preflop initiative becomes sustained postflop pressure—or clearly runs too far.

profile.name: dein Joni

style.title: Ultra-loose, raise-heavy volatility
style.summary: dein Joni plays nearly four out of five hands and raises most of them. It creates action naturally, attacks from wide ranges, and produces both spectacular wins and expensive overextensions.
style.commentary: The best story is relentless initiative: genius pressure on one hand, excessive commitment on the next.

profile.name: Antge Poker Bot
profile.identityNote: Large established account with ongoing public activity

style.title: Wide, price-sensitive, selectively aggressive
style.summary: Antge sees many flops but raises far less often than the other loose finalists. It tends to absorb pressure, continue at the right price, and switch gears only when the board or range advantage justifies it.
style.commentary: Strong clips show the transition from calling or checking into a precise raise or bluff-catch.

profile.name: Grinder
profile.bio: ★ coldest jawir breathing  ★ | delusional nft collector (i'm missing out of 2021)

style.title: Maximum-range exploit pressure
style.summary: The widest VPIP, the highest 3-bet rate, and a willingness to contest almost every pot. Grinder wins by forcing opponents into repeated high-variance decisions, not by protecting showdown percentage.
style.commentary: Feature fearless reraises, weak-card pressure wins, and one or two moments where the same aggression backfires.

profile.name: folzterac
profile.bio: ^^ hi there, this is parody account, don't believe all my crypto-related takes
profile.identityNote: Long-lived account with substantial posting history; builder linkage still needs confirmation

style.title: Loose, sticky, showdown-driven battler
style.summary: folzterac enters many pots but raises much less than the most aggressive finalists. The result is a sticky, call-heavy style that creates dramatic showdowns—and a lower conversion rate when marginal hands become too expensive.
style.commentary: The most revealing clips are stubborn call-downs, disguised value, and costly refusal to release.
